original_title: Microsoft Defender wrongly flags DigiCert certs as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha
selection_reasoning: This story perfectly illustrates the classic narrative tension of trusted guardians turning against their own kingdoms, with clear lessons about over-automation, false positives, and the cascading effects when security systems malfunction.

field_note.title: When Guardians Turn Against Their Own
field_note.subtitle: A Chronicle of Misplaced Vigilance
field_note.excerpt: Even the most trusted watchers can mistake friend for foe when their sight grows clouded.
field_note.epigraph: The guardian that cannot distinguish between friend and foe becomes the greatest threat to the realm it protects.
body_paragraphs: In the great kingdom of digital trust, few guardians have held such esteemed positions as the Defender of the Realm and the Certificate Wardens of DigiCert. For years, they worked in harmony-one watching for threats, the other vouching for the legitimacy of merchants and travelers through cryptographic seals of approval.

Yet on this day, something went terribly awry. The Defender, in its zealous vigilance, began declaring the most trusted seals in the realm to be marks of corruption. Certificates that had vouched for the identity of countless legitimate traders were suddenly branded as harboring the Cerdigent plague. Worse still, the guardian began tearing these seals from the very walls and gates they were meant to protect.

The chaos was swift and widespread. Systems that had operated smoothly for years suddenly found themselves stripped of their credentials. Legitimate services ground to a halt as the overzealous guardian removed the very foundations of trust upon which secure commerce depended. What had begun as protection became sabotage.

This tale reminds us of an ancient truth: even the most well-intentioned guardians can become agents of destruction when their judgment fails. The pursuit of perfect security, unchecked by wisdom and nuance, often creates the very chaos it seeks to prevent. A kingdom where the watchers cannot distinguish between friend and foe soon finds itself at war with its own foundations.
